components-alertdialog.name: AlertDialogContent

stories.name: Default
stories.snippet:
function Default() {
    return (
        <AlertDialog open>
            <AlertDialogContent size="default">
                <AlertDialogHeader>
                    <AlertDialogTitle>Delete workspace?</AlertDialogTitle>
                    <AlertDialogDescription>This action permanently removes the workspace and its saved settings.
                                  </AlertDialogDescription>
                </AlertDialogHeader>
                <AlertDialogFooter>
                    <AlertDialogCancel>Cancel</AlertDialogCancel>
                    <AlertDialogAction variant="destructive">Delete</AlertDialogAction>
                </AlertDialogFooter>
            </AlertDialogContent>
        </AlertDialog>
    );
}

stories.name: Small
stories.snippet:
function Small() {
    return (
        <AlertDialog open>
            <AlertDialogContent size="sm">
                <AlertDialogHeader>
                    <AlertDialogTitle>Delete workspace?</AlertDialogTitle>
                    <AlertDialogDescription>This action permanently removes the workspace and its saved settings.
                                  </AlertDialogDescription>
                </AlertDialogHeader>
                <AlertDialogFooter>
                    <AlertDialogCancel>Cancel</AlertDialogCancel>
                    <AlertDialogAction variant="destructive">Delete</AlertDialogAction>
                </AlertDialogFooter>
            </AlertDialogContent>
        </AlertDialog>
    );
}

Custom Items
Invalid State
stories.snippet:
const InvalidState = () => <Combobox items={frameworks}>
    <ComboboxInput
        showTrigger
        showClear={false}
        className="w-[240px]"
        placeholder="Choose a framework"
        aria-invalid />
    <ComboboxContent>
        <ComboboxEmpty>No frameworks found.</ComboboxEmpty>
        <ComboboxList>
            {(item) => (
              <ComboboxItem key={item} value={item}>
                {item}
              </ComboboxItem>
            )}
        </ComboboxList>
    </ComboboxContent>
</Combobox>;

stories.name: Disabled
stories.snippet:
const Disabled = () => <Combobox items={frameworks}>
    <ComboboxInput
        showTrigger
        showClear={false}
        className="w-[240px]"
        placeholder="Choose a framework"
        disabled />
    <ComboboxContent>
        <ComboboxEmpty>No frameworks found.</ComboboxEmpty>
        <ComboboxList>
            {(item) => (
              <ComboboxItem key={item} value={item}>
                {item}
              </ComboboxItem>
            )}
        </ComboboxList>
    </ComboboxContent>
</Combobox>;

stories.name: Auto Highlight
stories.snippet:
const AutoHighlight = () => <Combobox items={frameworks} autoHighlight>
    <ComboboxInput
        showTrigger
        showClear={false}
        className="w-[240px]"
        placeholder="Start typing..." />
    <ComboboxContent>
        <ComboboxEmpty>No frameworks found.</ComboboxEmpty>
        <ComboboxList>
            {(item) => (
              <ComboboxItem key={item} value={item}>
                {item}
              </ComboboxItem>
            )}
        </ComboboxList>
    </ComboboxContent>
</Combobox>;

components-contextmenu.name: ContextMenuItem

stories.name: Default
stories.snippet:
function Default() {
    const destructive = args?.variant === "destructive"

    return (
        <ContextMenu>
            <ContextMenuTrigger
                className="flex h-40 w-[320px] items-center justify-center rounded-xl border border-dashed text-sm text-muted-foreground">Right click this area
                      </ContextMenuTrigger>
            <ContextMenuContent className="w-52">
                <ContextMenuItem>
                    <PencilIcon />Rename
                              <ContextMenuShortcut>⌘R</ContextMenuShortcut>
                </ContextMenuItem>
                <ContextMenuItem>
                    <CopyIcon />Duplicate
                              <ContextMenuShortcut>⌘D</ContextMenuShortcut>
                </ContextMenuItem>
                <ContextMenuSeparator />
                <ContextMenuItem variant="default">
                    {destructive ? <Trash2Icon /> : <FolderIcon />}
                    {destructive ? "Delete" : "Move to folder"}
                </ContextMenuItem>
            </ContextMenuContent>
        </ContextMenu>
    );
}

stories.name: Destructive
stories.snippet:
function Destructive() {
    const destructive = args?.variant === "destructive"

    return (
        <ContextMenu>
            <ContextMenuTrigger
                className="flex h-40 w-[320px] items-center justify-center rounded-xl border border-dashed text-sm text-muted-foreground">Right click this area
                      </ContextMenuTrigger>
            <ContextMenuContent className="w-52">
                <ContextMenuItem>
                    <PencilIcon />Rename
                              <ContextMenuShortcut>⌘R</ContextMenuShortcut>
                </ContextMenuItem>
                <ContextMenuItem>
                    <CopyIcon />Duplicate
                              <ContextMenuShortcut>⌘D</ContextMenuShortcut>
                </ContextMenuItem>
                <ContextMenuSeparator />
                <ContextMenuItem variant="destructive">
                    {destructive ? <Trash2Icon /> : <FolderIcon />}
                    {destructive ? "Delete" : "Move to folder"}
                </ContextMenuItem>
            </ContextMenuContent>
        </ContextMenu>
    );
}

stories.name: With Trigger
stories.snippet:
const WithTrigger = () => (
  <Dialog>
    <DialogTrigger asChild>
      <Button variant="outline">Open Dialog</Button>
    </DialogTrigger>
    <DialogContent>
      <DialogHeader>
        <DialogTitle>Triggered dialog</DialogTitle>
        <DialogDescription>
          This dialog was opened via a trigger button.
        </DialogDescription>
      </DialogHeader>
      <DialogFooter>
        <DialogClose asChild>
          <Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button>
        </DialogClose>
        <Button>Confirm</Button>
      </DialogFooter>
    </DialogContent>
  </Dialog>
);

stories.name: Close Via Header Button
stories.snippet:
const CloseViaHeaderButton = () => (
  <Dialog>
    <DialogTrigger asChild>
      <Button variant="outline">Open</Button>
    </DialogTrigger>
    <DialogContent>
      <DialogHeader>
        <DialogTitle>Closable dialog</DialogTitle>
        <DialogDescription>
          Click the X button to close this dialog.
        </DialogDescription>
      </DialogHeader>
    </DialogContent>
  </Dialog>
);

components-menubar.name: MenubarItem

stories.name: Default
stories.snippet:
function Default() {
    const destructive = args?.variant === "destructive"

    return (
        <Menubar>
            <MenubarMenu>
                <MenubarTrigger>File</MenubarTrigger>
                <MenubarContent>
                    <MenubarItem>
                        <PencilIcon />Rename
                                    <MenubarShortcut>⌘R</MenubarShortcut>
                    </MenubarItem>
                    <MenubarItem>
                        <CopyIcon />Duplicate
                                    <MenubarShortcut>⌘D</MenubarShortcut>
                    </MenubarItem>
                    <MenubarSeparator />
                    <MenubarItem variant="default">
                        {destructive && <Trash2Icon />}
                        {destructive ? "Delete project" : "Archive project"}
                    </MenubarItem>
                </MenubarContent>
            </MenubarMenu>
            <MenubarMenu>
                <MenubarTrigger>Edit</MenubarTrigger>
                <MenubarContent>
                    <MenubarItem>Copy</MenubarItem>
                    <MenubarItem>Paste</MenubarItem>
                </MenubarContent>
            </MenubarMenu>
        </Menubar>
    );
}

stories.name: Destructive
stories.snippet:
function Destructive() {
    const destructive = args?.variant === "destructive"

    return (
        <Menubar>
            <MenubarMenu>
                <MenubarTrigger>File</MenubarTrigger>
                <MenubarContent>
                    <MenubarItem>
                        <PencilIcon />Rename
                                    <MenubarShortcut>⌘R</MenubarShortcut>
                    </MenubarItem>
                    <MenubarItem>
                        <CopyIcon />Duplicate
                                    <MenubarShortcut>⌘D</MenubarShortcut>
                    </MenubarItem>
                    <MenubarSeparator />
                    <MenubarItem variant="destructive">
                        {destructive && <Trash2Icon />}
                        {destructive ? "Delete project" : "Archive project"}
                    </MenubarItem>
                </MenubarContent>
            </MenubarMenu>
            <MenubarMenu>
                <MenubarTrigger>Edit</MenubarTrigger>
                <MenubarContent>
                    <MenubarItem>Copy</MenubarItem>
                    <MenubarItem>Paste</MenubarItem>
                </MenubarContent>
            </MenubarMenu>
        </Menubar>
    );
}

stories.snippet: const MockupMatch = () => <Chromatogram data={dnaSequenceData} />;

stories.name: With Explicit Bases

stories.name: Custom Colors
Single Trace
stories.description: Basic single trace chromatogram - the simplest usage of the component.

stories.name: Multiple Traces
stories.description: Overlay multiple injections to compare retention times and peak intensities.

stories.name: With Metadata
stories.description: Series with injection metadata displayed in tooltips.

stories.name: Peak Detection
stories.description: Automatic peak detection with area calculations using trapezoidal integration.

stories.name: Full Featured
stories.description: Full featured chromatogram combining all major features.

stories.name: With Boundary Markers
stories.description: Peak boundary markers showing triangle markers at peak start and diamond markers with vertical lines at peak end (the default styling).

stories.name: User Defined Peak Boundaries
stories.description: User-defined peaks with boundary information. Users can provide their own peak annotations with startIndex and endIndex to display boundary markers without using automatic peak detection.

stories.name: Combined Auto And User Peaks
stories.description: Combining automatic peak detection with user-defined annotations. Auto-detected peaks show computed areas while user annotations provide custom labels. Both can have boundary markers displayed.

series.description: Array of data series to display

markerSize.description: Marker size when showMarkers is true (default: 4)

peakDetectionOptions.description: Peak detection algorithm options - if provided, enables automatic peak detection

showPeakAreas.description: Show peak areas as annotations (default: false)

boundaryMarkers.description:
Show peak boundary markers (default: "none").
- "none": No boundary markers displayed
- "enabled": Show boundary markers using per-peak startMarker/endMarker settings

Per-peak marker defaults: startMarker="triangle", endMarker="diamond"

annotationOverlapThreshold.description:
Retention time threshold for grouping overlapping annotations (default: 0.4 minutes).
Peaks closer than this threshold will have their annotations staggered to avoid overlap.

showExportButton.description: Show export button in modebar (default: true)

stories.name: Default
Stacked
With Custom Colors
Large Markers
Multiple Series Colors
Small Scale
High Density
Real Data Visualization
Well Plate Mockup
Default Plate
Small Plate

deprecated: The Heatmap component is now a wrapper around PlateMap. For new projects, use PlateMap directly which provides more features including: - Plate format presets (96, 384 wells) - Well ID-based data input - Click handlers with metadata - Custom tooltip formatting

colorscale.description: Color scale - string name or array of [position, color] pairs

precision.description: Number of decimal places for values

stories.name: Default
With Custom Color
With Custom Bins
Stacked Histogram
Multiple Series
With Distribution Line
With Custom Bins And Distribution Line
Multiple Series With Distribution Lines

charts-interactivescatter.name: InteractiveScatter

stories.name: Basic Scatter
stories.description: Minimal scatter plot — just data and axis titles, everything else defaults.

stories.name: Data Driven Styling
stories.description: Color mapped to `category` (categorical), shape to `status` (categorical), size to `intensity` (continuous) — all driven by point metadata.

stories.name: Static Styling
stories.description: Fixed color, shape, and size — ignores data values entirely.

stories.name: Selection
stories.description: All three selection modes enabled: click a point, drag a box, or draw a lasso. The selected count is shown below the chart.

stories.name: Keyboard Modifier Selection
stories.description: Keyboard modifiers apply to click selection only. Click = replace, Shift+Click = add, Ctrl/Cmd+Click = remove, Shift+Ctrl/Cmd+Click = toggle. Box/lasso selection always replaces. The active mode is shown below.

stories.name: Selection With Data Grid
stories.description: Select points and a data grid to the right updates in real time.

stories.name: Custom Tooltips
stories.description: Uses `tooltip.content` to render rich HTML tooltips with status badges.

stories.name: Axis Fixed Ranges
stories.description: Fixed axis ranges zoom the viewport to a specific data region.

stories.name: Axis Log Scale
stories.description: Log-scale axes for data spanning several orders of magnitude.

stories.name: Downsampling
stories.description: 10k points downsampled to 1k via LTTB to keep the chart responsive. You can upload your own data file to test downsampling: - **CSV** — must have `x` and `y` columns. Optional: `id`, `label`. Any extra columns become `metadata`. ``` id,x,y,label,category,value p-0,2.3,4.1,Alpha,Group A,120.5 ``` - **JSON** — array of objects with at least `x` and `y` fields. ``` [{ "id": "p-0", "x": 2.3, "y": 4.1, "label": "Alpha", "category": "Group A" }] ```

charts-interactivescatter.description: InteractiveScatter component for visualizing scatter plot data with advanced interactions. **Features:** - Data-driven and static styling (color, shape, size) - Interactive selection (click, box, lasso) - Keyboard modifiers for click-selection modes (Shift/Ctrl) - Customizable tooltips with rich content support - Axis customization (ranges, log/linear scales) - Performance optimizations for large datasets - Selection propagation via callbacks **Selection Modes (click selection only):** - Default click: Replace selection - Shift + click: Add to selection - Ctrl/Cmd + click: Remove from selection - Shift + Ctrl + click: Toggle selection Box/lasso selection always replaces the current selection because Plotly does not expose the original keyboard event for drag operations.

reactDocgen.description:
InteractiveScatter component for visualizing scatter plot data with advanced interactions.

**Features:**
- Data-driven and static styling (color, shape, size)
- Interactive selection (click, box, lasso)
- Keyboard modifiers for click-selection modes (Shift/Ctrl)
- Customizable tooltips with rich content support
- Axis customization (ranges, log/linear scales)
- Performance optimizations for large datasets
- Selection propagation via callbacks

**Selection Modes (click selection only):**
- Default click: Replace selection
- Shift + click: Add to selection
- Ctrl/Cmd + click: Remove from selection
- Shift + Ctrl + click: Toggle selection

Box/lasso selection always replaces the current selection because
Plotly does not expose the original keyboard event for drag operations.

data.description: Array of data points to plot

enableBoxSelection.description:
Enable box selection (drag to select rectangular region)
@default true

selectedIds.description:
Controlled selection state.
If provided, component operates in controlled mode.

type.name: union
type.raw:
| "replace" // Default: Replace existing selection
| "add" // Shift: Add to selection
| "remove" // Ctrl: Remove from selection
| "toggle"

onPointClick.description: Callback when a point is clicked

charts-platemap.name: PlateMap

stories.name: Plate 96 Well
stories.description: 96-well plate with WellData array input Demonstrates the recommended data format with well IDs, values, and tooltipData

stories.name: Plate 384 Well
stories.description: 384-well plate with WellData array input Demonstrates larger plate format with 16 rows × 24 columns

stories.name: Plate 1536 Well
stories.description: 1536-well plate with double-letter row notation (AA-AF) Demonstrates high-density plate format with 32 rows × 48 columns

stories.name: Custom Dimensions
stories.description: Custom plate dimensions Demonstrates using custom rows/columns for non-standard plates

stories.name: Partial Plate
stories.description: Partial plate with empty wells Demonstrates handling of sparse data and empty wells

stories.name: Generic Heatmap
stories.description: Generic heatmap with axis titles Demonstrates using PlateMap as a general-purpose heatmap with custom axis labels

stories.name: Random Data
stories.description: Auto-generated random data Demonstrates PlateMap with no data - generates random values automatically

stories.name: Categorical Visualization
stories.description: Categorical visualization mode Demonstrates displaying well types with discrete colors

stories.name: Layer Toggling
stories.description: Multi-layer data with layer toggling Demonstrates wells with multiple values and layer switching

stories.name: Region Highlighting
stories.description: Region highlighting with PlateRegions Demonstrates rectangular region overlays with borders and fill colors

stories.name: Custom Category Colors
stories.description: Custom category colors Demonstrates overriding default categorical colors

stories.name: Mixed Value Types
stories.description: Mixed value types (numeric and categorical) Demonstrates layers with both heatmap and categorical visualization

stories.name: Legend Configuration
stories.description: Legend configuration options Demonstrates legend positioning and styling

stories.name: Hidden UI Elements
stories.description: Hidden UI elements (showColorBar=false, showLegend=false) Tests rendering without colorbar and legend, including horizontal legend position

stories.name: Edge Cases
stories.description: Edge cases: NaN/Infinity values, null values Tests graceful handling of invalid numeric values (converted to null)

stories.name: Uniform Values And Left Legend
stories.description: Uniform values: all wells have the same value (min === max edge case) Also tests left legend position for heatmap data

stories.name: Categorical Left Legend
stories.description: Categorical data with left legend position Tests left legend rendering code path (lines 1121, 1471-1475)

stories.name: Well Click Interaction
stories.description: Well click interaction Tests the onWellClick callback by simulating Plotly click events

stories.name: String Colorscale
stories.description: Named Plotly colorscale (string format) Tests using a string colorscale like "Viridis" instead of array

stories.name: String Colorscale With Empty Wells
stories.description: Named colorscale with empty wells Tests that empty wells display with emptyWellColor when using named colorscales (string format) This regression test covers the bug where empty wells showed as the minimum color of the scale

stories.name: Value Range Override
stories.description: Custom value range overrides Tests valueMin and valueMax props to override auto-calculated range

stories.name: Colorbar With Title
stories.description: Colorbar with title (heatmap legend title) Tests legendConfig.title with heatmap mode

stories.name: Invalid Regions
stories.description: Invalid regions: tests graceful handling of invalid/out-of-bounds region definitions Covers invalid well format and regions extending beyond plate dimensions

charts-platemap.description: PlateMap component for visualizing well plate data as a heatmap or categorical display. **Supported Plate Formats:** - 96-well (8 rows × 12 columns, wells A1-H12) - 384-well (16 rows × 24 columns, wells A1-P24) - 1536-well (32 rows × 48 columns, wells A1-AF48) - Custom dimensions with user-specified rows/columns **Visualization Modes:** - `"heatmap"`: Continuous color gradient for quantitative values - `"categorical"`: Discrete colors for well types (sample, control, empty) **Features:** - Multiple data layers with independent visualization settings - Control region highlighting with borders and fill colors - Configurable color scales, tooltips, and click interactions - Support for WellData arrays with multi-layer visualization **Data Format:** - **WellData array**: `[{ wellId: "A1", values: { RFU: 100 }, tooltipData: {...} }, ...]`

reactDocgen.description:
PlateMap component for visualizing well plate data as a heatmap or categorical display.

**Supported Plate Formats:**
- 96-well (8 rows × 12 columns, wells A1-H12)
- 384-well (16 rows × 24 columns, wells A1-P24)
- 1536-well (32 rows × 48 columns, wells A1-AF48)
- Custom dimensions with user-specified rows/columns

**Visualization Modes:**
- `"heatmap"`: Continuous color gradient for quantitative values
- `"categorical"`: Discrete colors for well types (sample, control, empty)

**Features:**
- Multiple data layers with independent visualization settings
- Control region highlighting with borders and fill colors
- Configurable color scales, tooltips, and click interactions
- Support for WellData arrays with multi-layer visualization

**Data Format:**
- **WellData array**: `[{ wellId: "A1", values: { RFU: 100 }, tooltipData: {...} }, ...]`

data.description:
Well data array as WellData objects with wellId and values.

If wells have multiple values (via `values` property), layers are
auto-generated for each unique key, enabling layer toggling.

layerConfigs.description:
Configuration for auto-generated layers when using WellData with `values`.
Use this to customize display names, colors, and ranges for each layer.
Only used when `data` contains wells with `values` property.

initialLayerId.description:
Initial layer ID to display when the component mounts.
If not provided, defaults to the first layer.

onLayerChange.description:
Optional callback notified when the active layer changes.
This is purely informational - the component manages layer state internally.
Use this for logging, analytics, or syncing with external state.

tsType.name: union
tsType.raw:
| typeof PLATE_FORMAT_96
| typeof PLATE_FORMAT_384
| typeof PLATE_FORMAT_1536
| typeof PLATE_FORMAT_CUSTOM

rows.description: Number of rows for custom format (default: 8 for 96-well, 16 for 384-well)

columns.description: Number of columns for custom format (default: 12 for 96-well, 24 for 384-well)

visualizationMode.description:
Visualization mode (default: "heatmap")
- "heatmap": Display quantitative values with color gradient
- "categorical": Display well types with discrete colors

categoryColors.description:
Custom colors for categorical visualization mode.
Keys are well types, values are hex colors.
Merged with DEFAULT_CATEGORY_COLORS.

regions.description:
Regions to highlight on the plate (e.g., controls, sample areas, empty wells).
Each region can specify wells and styling for visual distinction.

xLabels.description: Custom x-axis labels (overrides auto-generated column numbers)

yLabels.description: Custom y-axis labels (overrides auto-generated row letters)

colorScale.description: Color scale for the heatmap (only used in heatmap mode)

valueMin.description: Minimum value for color scale (auto-calculated if not provided)

valueMax.description: Maximum value for color scale (auto-calculated if not provided)

showColorBar.description: Show color bar legend for heatmap mode (default: true)

showLegend.description: Show categorical legend for categorical mode (default: true)

precision.description: Number of decimal places for values (default: 0)

markerShape.description:
Marker shape for wells (default: "circle")
- "circle": Round markers, ideal for plate-based data
- "square": Square markers, ideal for generic heatmaps

onWellClick.description:
Callback when a well/cell is clicked.
@param wellData - The full well data object including wellId, values, and tooltipData

stories.snippet: const CustomRanges = () => <ScatterGraph xRange={[300, 700]} yRange={[0, 200]} />;

tetradata-platform-search-server.name: TdpSearch

stories.name: Basic Usage
stories.snippet:
const BasicUsage = () => <TdpSearch
    apiEndpoint="/api/search"
    columns={[
      { key: "id", header: "ID", width: "120px" },
      { key: "filePath", header: "File Path", sortable: true },
      { key: "sourceType", header: "Source Type", sortable: true },
      { key: "status", header: "Status", sortable: true },
      { key: "fileSize", header: "Size", sortable: true },
      { key: "createdAt", header: "Created", sortable: true },
    ]}
    defaultQuery="sample-data"
    searchPlaceholder="Search files..." />;

stories.name: Full Featured
With Filters
Custom Rendering
stories.description: Demonstrates per-column custom renderers: colored status badges, source-type pills, monospace file paths, and human-readable sizes/dates.

stories.name: Custom Search Bar
stories.description: Custom search bar via renderSearchBar — TdpSearch manages all state; only the input UI is swapped.

example: ```tsx <TdpSearch columns={[ { key: "id", header: "ID" }, { key: "filePath", header: "File Path", sortable: true } ]} defaultQuery="sample-data" pageSize={20} /> ```

tetradata-platform-search-server.description: TdpSearch Component A reusable search component for querying the TDP.

columns.description: Display fields/columns for the results table

filters.description: UI filters displayed as dropdowns (for user selection)

advancedSearchParams.description:
Additional search parameters to pass to the SDK's searchEql method.
Allows customization of expression, selectedPipelineIds, selectedSourceTypes, etc.

onSearch.description: Callback fired when search is executed with the query and results

renderSearchBar.description: Replace the entire search bar (input + button)

standalone.description:
When true, calls the TDP API directly from the browser
When false/omitted, proxies the request through your backend `apiEndpoint`

baseUrl.description: TDP API base URL (required when standalone is true)

apiEndpoint.description: Backend API endpoint for search (used when standalone is false). Defaults to '/api/search'

example: ```tsx import { TDPLink, tdpPaths } from '@tetrascience-npm/tetrascience-react-ui'; <TDPLink path={tdpPaths.fileDetails("abc-123")}> View File Details </TDPLink> <TDPLink path="/search?q=test" navigationOptions={{ newTab: false }}> Search in TDP (same tab) </TDPLink> ```

tetradata-platform-link.description: A link component that navigates to TDP pages. Renders a standard `<a>` tag with the correct href for right-click "Open in new tab" support, accessibility, and SEO. Must be used inside a `<TdpNavigationProvider>`.

reactDocgen.description:
A link component that navigates to TDP pages.

Renders a standard `<a>` tag with the correct href for right-click
"Open in new tab" support, accessibility, and SEO.

Must be used inside a `<TdpNavigationProvider>`.

@example
```tsx
import { TDPLink, tdpPaths } from '@tetrascience-npm/tetrascience-react-ui';

<TDPLink path={tdpPaths.fileDetails("abc-123")}>
  View File Details
</TDPLink>

<TDPLink path="/search?q=test" navigationOptions={{ newTab: false }}>
  Search in TDP (same tab)
</TDPLink>
```

path.description: TDP page path (e.g., "/file/abc-123" or use tdpPaths helpers)
